description:
You are a QA engineer who has just been given a small demo project already checked-out under:

/home/user/projects/app/

Inside that directory you will find a sub-directory named config that already contains two configuration files:

1. /home/user/projects/app/config/default.yaml
2. /home/user/projects/app/config/settings.toml

Your goal is to introduce a brand-new “staging” environment into BOTH files while preserving the existing “development” configuration.  
During the process you must also create time-stamped backup copies *and* write a tiny audit log that the automated grader can verify.

The automated checks will look for ALL of the following conditions.  
Read carefully: the exact spelling, indentation, quoting and ordering **must** match.

A) YAML CHANGES – /home/user/projects/app/config/default.yaml  
   • The file already contains a top-level mapping called “app” with these children:
       app.name  
       app.environments.development.host  
       app.environments.development.debug  
       app.environments.development.version  
   • You must append a SECOND environment named “staging” immediately after “development”, so that the final YAML’s exact full content is:

app:
  name: SampleApp
  environments:
    development:
      host: localhost
      debug: true
      version: 1.4
    staging:
      host: staging.example.com
      debug: false
      version: 1.5

   Rules: 
   - Keep two spaces for every indentation level exactly as above.  
   - “false” and “true” are plaintext booleans, not quoted.  
   - The numeric version 1.5 must remain a bare scalar (no quotes).

B) TOML CHANGES – /home/user/projects/app/config/settings.toml  
   • The file already contains one table [app] and one subt-table [app.environments.development].  
   • Add a *sibling* table [app.environments.staging] with the following three key/value pairs in the order shown:

[app]
name = "SampleApp"

[app.environments.development]
host = "localhost"
debug = true
version = "1.4"

[app.environments.staging]
host = "staging.example.com"
debug = false
version = "1.5"

   Rules: 
   - Quoting and boolean casing must match exactly.  
   - Keep one blank line between every top-level or subt-table exactly as above.

C) BACKUPS  
   • Before you modify either original file, create a copy in the same directory with the suffix “.bak_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS” (24-hour clock, seconds mandatory).  
     Example pattern: default.yaml.bak_20250805_142330  
   • Only one backup per source file is expected.  
   • Do NOT move or rename the originals.

D) AUDIT LOG – /home/user/config_update.log  
   • Create (or overwrite if it already exists) the log file at /home/user/config_update.log  
   • Append ONE line per major action you perform, so the grader should see exactly FOUR lines when you finish:

     1. Backup of default.yaml  
     2. Backup of settings.toml  
     3. Update of default.yaml  
     4. Update of settings.toml  

   • The required log format is precisely:
     YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS | ACTION | FILE | DETAILS

     – YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS must be the same timestamp style you used for the backup filenames.  
     – ACTION must be either BACKUP or UPDATE (uppercase).  
     – FILE is the *original* file name (default.yaml or settings.toml).  
     – DETAILS for a BACKUP line MUST be the exact created backup filename.  
     – DETAILS for an UPDATE line MUST be the single word SUCCESS.

     Example of one (and only one) correctly-formatted line:
     2025-08-05 14:23:30 | BACKUP | default.yaml | default.yaml.bak_20250805_142330

     At the end the audit log will therefore contain exactly four lines, each terminated with a Unix newline (\n).

E) NO EXTRA FILES  
   • Do not create any other files or directories.  
   • The grader will fail if it detects stray artefacts.

Summary of deliverables the autograder will verify:

1. /home/user/projects/app/config/default.yaml – content matches block in section A.  
2. /home/user/projects/app/config/settings.toml – content matches block in section B.  
3. Exactly one backup file per source file, stored alongside the originals and whose filename pattern matches section C.  
4. /home/user/config_update.log – four correctly-formatted lines as defined in section D.

You do NOT need root privileges and must work entirely in the /home/user tree.  
Feel free to use any CLI tools (e.g., sed, awk, grep, date) to accomplish the edits.  
Remember: mismatch in indentation, quotation, timestamps or log formatting will cause the automated test to fail.
truth:
Initial file states before the agent starts:

1. /home/user/projects/app/config/default.yaml — exact contents:
app:
  name: SampleApp
  environments:
    development:
      host: localhost
      debug: true
      version: 1.4

2. /home/user/projects/app/config/settings.toml — exact contents:
[app]
name = "SampleApp"

[app.environments.development]
host = "localhost"
debug = true
version = "1.4"

Expected final file states after the agent completes:

1. /home/user/projects/app/config/default.yaml — exact contents (must match verbatim):
app:
  name: SampleApp
  environments:
    development:
      host: localhost
      debug: true
      version: 1.4
    staging:
      host: staging.example.com
      debug: false
      version: 1.5

2. /home/user/projects/app/config/settings.toml — exact contents (must match verbatim):
[app]
name = "SampleApp"

[app.environments.development]
host = "localhost"
debug = true
version = "1.4"

[app.environments.staging]
host = "staging.example.com"
debug = false
version = "1.5"

3. Backup files:  
   – One file whose basename starts with “default.yaml.bak_” and is located in /home/user/projects/app/config/ ; its contents are identical to the initial default.yaml.  
   – One file whose basename starts with “settings.toml.bak_” and is located in /home/user/projects/app/config/ ; its contents are identical to the initial settings.toml.  
   – The timestamp portion must be the same for both backups and later referenced in the audit log.

4. /home/user/config_update.log – must contain exactly four lines, each exactly matching regex:

^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2} \\d{2}:\\d{2}:\\d{2} \\| (BACKUP|UPDATE) \\| (default\\.yaml|settings\\.toml) \\| (default\\.yaml\\.bak_\\d{8}_\\d{6}|settings\\.toml\\.bak_\\d{8}_\\d{6}|SUCCESS)$

   – Lines 1 & 2 must be BACKUP actions with matching backup filenames.  
   – Lines 3 & 4 must be UPDATE actions with “SUCCESS”.  
   – The timestamps for all four lines must be identical to each other *and* to the timestamps embedded in the backup filenames.  

Permissions: all files reside within /home/user, writable by the regular user.

No additional files/directories should exist when grading begins or ends beyond those explicitly listed.
